data.title: As a non-engineer user, the installer clones the template, substitutes my project name, installs npm deps, and writes a completion sentinel
data.description:
Done Definition:
- plugins/dso/scripts/create-dso-app.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x applied at creation)
- Script clones the template fork with git clone --no-single-branch <repo_url> to ensure the tickets orphan branch is fetched alongside the default branch
- Project name substitution: replaces all {{PROJECT_NAME}} placeholders across all template files using sed; shell-unsafe characters (spaces, slashes, $, *, ?, [, ], \, ^) are sanitized (stripped/replaced with hyphens) or rejected with an actionable error message before sed runs
- npm install runs after clone; stdout is suppressed unless exit code != 0
- .dso-init-complete sentinel file written to project root only after all steps succeed (never written on partial completion)
- Script is wrapped in a main() function invoked as the last line of the file (partial-download protection — prevents partial execution if pipe closes mid-download)
- All tests in tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh covering S3 behavior pass (GREEN)

Considerations:
- curl | bash feeds the script via stdin; exec < /dev/tty mid-script may truncate the script on some bash versions if bash is also reading the script from stdin. Canonical invocation to recommend: bash <(curl -sL url) using process substitution — bash reads script from a file descriptor, so exec < /dev/tty works safely. The SC7 curl example and installer README MUST use the bash <(curl) form, not curl | bash.

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
